description: A review task preserves evidence by avoiding source changes.
body:
In review mode, the useful artifact is an evidence-backed assessment. Editing files during review can invalidate the diff being reviewed and mix remediation with assessment.

Evidence boundary: apply only when current task, repository, or validation evidence matches this pack boundary. Do not override a more specific pack or explicit project contract. Use when Code Review work touches diff hunks, line evidence, tests, contracts, and blast radius. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include file/line citations, failing or missing tests, reproduced behavior, and residual-risk notes. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves unsupported findings, severity inflation, hidden fixes, and ignoring user constraints, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
